scope_note: QA Norm-Flip Signed-Temporal Cert family [214]: The Eisenstein quadratic form f(b,e)=b*b+b*e-e*e satisfies the integer identity f(T(b,e))=-f(b,e) where T(b,e)=(e,b+e). This gives the T-orbit graph a natural signed-temporal structure: at each integer path-time step the sign of the unreduced norm flips. Corollary: T^2 preserves f mod m. On S_9, T-orbits decompose into 5 signed orbit families exactly matching the Pythagorean Families paper: Fibonacci (size 24, norm pair {1,8}), Lucas (24, {4,5}), Phibonacci (24, {2,7}), Tribonacci satellite (8, {0}), Ninbonacci singularity (1, {0}). The three cosmos orbits are bipartite signed (two cohorts of 12 each); satellite and singularity form the null subgraph where f is identically zero mod 9. Connects [133] Eisenstein norm identities, [155] Bearden phase conjugate (phase conjugation IS norm-sign flip), [191] Bateson cosmos/satellite/singularity stratification, [211] Cayley, [212] Fibonacci Hypergraph, [213] Causal DAG — this is the signed/temporal view of the same (b,e,d,a) object, completing slots 1-5 of the graph types initiative. Source: Eisenstein 1844 (ternary quadratic forms), Pythagorean Families paper (qa formalization), Bearden scalar EM, Sixto two-branch packet work.
